channel.title: BBC News
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Pence calls Trump's 'anti-weaponisation' fund 'deeply offensive' and says it should be dropped
item.description: Trump's former vice-president, who was targeted by rioters during the January 6 riots, says the fund that could potentially compensate them is a "bad idea".

item.title: 'Cancel it', Trump says after artists drop out of US Freedom 250 festival
item.description: Trump suggests holding a "Make America Great Again" rally instead after most headliners drop out of White-House-linked event.

item.title: Bus driver in deadly Virginia crash charged with involuntary manslaughter
item.description: Police say Jing S Dong was driving a tour bus in a "criminally negligent manner" when he plowed into cars and killed five people.

item.title: US, UK and Australia to develop underwater drone technology
item.description: The technology, developed under the Aukus military pact, is aimed at protecting undersea cables and boosting naval defence.

item.title: Trump in 'excellent health' but should lose weight and exercise more, his doctor says
item.description: The White House releases the results of the US president's latest medical examination, which states he is "fully fit" to carry out his duties.

item.title: Oscar-winning Star Wars editor Marcia Lucas dies aged 80
item.description: The film editor and ex-wife of director George Lucas was widely recognised as a pivotal creative force behind the original space trilogy.

item.title: US not 'turning back' on Asia allies, but expects them to boost defence, says Hegseth
item.description: The US defense secretary is questioned about US commitment, including further arms deals, at an Asian security summit in Singapore.

item.title: Ex-US attorney general defends Epstein files handling in congressional probe
item.description: The testimony comes about a month after America's top prosecutor was ousted by US President Donald Trump.

item.title: No deal announced after Trump meeting to make 'final determination' on Iran
item.description: The US president met his advisers, after officials confirmed the US and Iran had agreed a framework of a deal.

item.title: 'Poison seller' who sold toxic chemicals online to people across world admits aiding suicides
item.description: Kenneth Law admitted charges relating to Canadian victims - but families say he should also be charged in the UK over 79 deaths in Britain.

item.title: US judge orders Trump's name be removed from Kennedy Center
item.description: The performing arts centre must also remain open despite Trump announcing a two-year closure for extensive renovations.

item.title: Exploding rocket casts doubts over Nasa's Moon plans
item.description: Explosion of Blue Origin rocket is a setback for the company and for Nasa's Moon plans.

item.title: Marilyn Monroe 'lookalikes' gather to celebrate her 100th birthday
item.description: Fans set a new world record for the largest gathering of people dressed as Marilyn Monroe to celebrate what would have been the legendary actress’ centenary.

item.title: What does Blue Origin rocket mishap mean for Nasa's Moon mission?
item.description: Science correspondent Pallab Ghosh explains why the explosion is a setback for space exploration.

item.title: Are the US and Iran close to reaching a peace deal… again?
item.description: BBC’s Sarah Smith looks at why both countries are in a tense cycle of negotiations, despite the progress President Trump consistently claims.

item.title: Watch: Moment 14-year-old wins US national spelling bee
item.description: A teenager from California has won the 2026 Scripps National Spelling Bee, after days of fierce competition.

item.title: Why Fifa is being investigated over World Cup ticket prices
item.description: New York and New Jersey are looking into the association after fans have reportedly been "misled" over ticket sales and seat locations.

item.title: Watch: Community gathers as workers remain missing after chemical blast
item.description: At least one person is dead and nine others missing after a chemical explosion at a paper mill in the US state of Washington, authorities said.

item.title: Trump's doctor says he's in excellent health - but are US presidents' health checks a PR exercise?
item.description: Every president in modern history - including Donald Trump this week - has taken an annual physical exam that is as much about messaging as it is about health.

item.title: 'Men written by women': How ice hockey romances like Off Campus became TV's hottest new genre
item.description: The Amazon Prime adaptation of Elle Kennedy's ice hockey romance book is an early hit with viewers.

item.title: California teen wins US spelling bee - but could you compete? Take our quiz
item.description: Nine kids have battled it out in the 2026 Scripps National Spelling Bee finals - can you spell as well as they did?

item.title: When trade soured, this American liquor maker moved to Canada
item.description: Sales of the fruity liqueur popular with Canadian students had plummetted north of the border as provinces retaliated against Trump tariffs.

item.title: MAGA candidate's win in Texas primary may also help Democrats in Senate battle
item.description: Ken Paxton's victory sets up what will be a closely watched contest in November's midterm elections.
